name: Grignard Reaction
description: A Grignard reagent forms when magnesium inserts into a carbon-halogen bond, giving a strongly nucleophilic carbanion equivalent that adds to electrophilic carbonyls (or CO2); aqueous workup then protonates the alkoxide to give an alcohol or carboxylic acid.
